MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Nelson V. Goodrich, Et Al - Defendant Allianz Notice Of Motion And Msj
Off Calendar Jun-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Norman Nelson.
Judiee Pinkerton of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for intervenor and moving party Allianz Insurance Company, on behalf of its' Suspended, Insured, Defendant Sudgen Engineering Co.

RULING: INTERVENOR ALLIANZ INSURANCE COMPANY, ON BEHALF OF ITS' SUSPENDED, INSURED, DEFENDANT SUDGEN ENGINEERING CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenor Allianz Insurance Company, on Behalf of Its' Suspended, Insured, Defendant Sudgen Engineering Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) For instance, Defendant asked no follow up questions regarding Plaintiff's testimony that the name Sudgen rings a bell. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kelly Roemer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 6435, kroemer@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580). (503/CML)

Off Calendar Jun-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Norman Nelson.
Thao Pham of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000x237), appearing for defendant and moving party Allied Heating & Air Conditioning Co., Inc.

RULING: DEFENDANT ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, matter not argued for late notice by moving party to contest tentative ruling and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Allied Heating & Air Conditioning Co., Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) For instance, Defendant asked no questions regarding Plaintiff's alleged exposure at the Metropolitan Life Building in San Francisco from 1972 to 1973. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Plaintiff's objection to Defendant's Exhibit L (printout from the California Secretary of State's website) is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kelly Roemer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 6435, kroemer@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580). (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Brought By Keenan Properties, Inc.
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Norman Nelson.

RULING: DEFENDANT KEENAN PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No appearance by moving party and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Keenan Properties, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) For instance, Defendant asked no follow up questions regarding Plaintiff's testimony that he handled Keenan deliveries. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Defendant's objection to Plaintiff's Ex. B to the Declaration of Lloyd F. Leroy and Plaintiff's affirmative use of his responses to written discovery is sustained. Defendant's objection to Plaintiff's deposition testimony is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
